clinical_history: A 64 year old man with a history of hypertension presents with palpitations. What is the ECG diagnosis?
diagnosis: The ECG shows a very regular, narrow complex (normal QRS duration) tachycardia.  The pattern is that of typical, counterclockwise atrial flutter with 2:1 AV block (conduction). The atrial rate is 270 cycles/min and the QRS (effective ventricular) rate is 135 beats/min. Note the negative flutter (F, not true P) waves in the inferior limb leads and positive flutter waves in V1 and V2. Every other F wave is partly hidden in the end of the QRS/early ST segment.  Nonspecific ST-T changes are also present
